findings.problem: The Kai-section close third states Margot's pleasure motive and the full Lena-pool betrayal as settled fact before Margot has confessed it on-page. Lena's off-page briefing in line 153 gives logs and suspension, but line 155 jumps to Kai 'knowing' that the dissolution was a rush for Margot; then line 181 has him confronting her with the same conclusion as if the inference chain were already earned. The scene arrives pre-solved, which weakens the confrontation's reveal and breaks local POV-legibility.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:153 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:155 so the text distinguishes what Lena actually told Kai from what he is inferring. Then adjust snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:181 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183 so Margot's admission that the desync improved the feed is either the first confirmed statement of motive or is explicitly grounded in an earlier quoted disclosure.
findings.acceptance_test: Before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183, a cold reader can name which Lena-details Kai has been told and which ones he only suspects. The revised scene fails if Kai still presents Margot's pleasure motive as established fact before the text has shown who gave him that information.

findings.problem: The chapter is supposed to expose Margot's feed addiction as ugly, bodily compulsion, but most of the rendering is explanatory or retrospective. The opening gives ritual and numbness, then line 19 says the thing between her and the device is 'already over,' which defuses present addictive pressure. The dock confession explains thresholds, intensity, and lost competition, yet it still reads more like a lucid diagnosis than the immediate misery of wanting the thing that wrecked other people. That is sanitization by summary: the addiction becomes legible without staying physically unbearable on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:203, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:223, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:227 to add a present-time bodily pull toward the interface/feed: compulsive handling, anticipatory body-memory, disgust at the missing response, or another concrete craving beat. If line 19 remains, the surrounding text needs to make clear that the dead pulse is shock or trauma numbness, not the extinction of desire.
findings.acceptance_test: A reader can point to at least one specific present-tense bodily craving beat in the revised opening or dock scene, not just explanatory sentences about why the feed mattered. The revision fails if Margot still sounds chiefly analytical about the addiction and the page never renders what her body wants right now.

findings.problem: Both major confrontations lean on the same interrogation ladder: accusation, clipped acknowledgment, sharper accusation, clipped acknowledgment, then a confession. That repeated turn-shape makes Petra's institutional suspension scene and Kai's personal confrontation feel too similar in conversational mechanics, and it flattens voice separation because both scenes use Margot as the same yes/no confirmation machine.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise either snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:79 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:87 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:193, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:237 so one confrontation runs on a different pressure-pattern. Petra can use hedged medical precision or self-correction; Kai can circle, misstate, stop, or shift physically before landing the question. Keep Margot stripped-down, but stop giving her long runs of bare confirmations as the engine of both scenes.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised Petra scene and Kai scene back-to-back, a cold reader can hear materially different conversational mechanics, not just different information. The revision fails if either scene still advances mainly through three or more consecutive accusation-confirmation beats with Margot answering in one-beat assent.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into the same negation-and-correction scaffold: 'Not as data... but as sensation,' 'Not because... Because,' 'Not sporadic, not variable,' 'Not composed. Flat,' 'Not a question. Not exactly a demand,' 'Not a simulation.' Once that structure appears across multiple scenes and both narration and dialogue-adjacent summary, it reads as a chapter tic rather than a chosen emphasis pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence strategy in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:203. Let some beats land through direct sensory detail, motion, or image instead of another negation/redefinition turn. Keep the intensity, but stop solving emphasis with the same syntactic move each time.
findings.acceptance_test: At least two of the cited passages are revised so they no longer rely on a paired negation/redefinition scaffold to land the beat. The revision fails if the opening, Petra scene, and dock scene still all use the same 'not X / but Y' or 'not X. Y.' cadence as their signature emphasis.

findings.problem: The dock scene lands its core meaning by the end of Margot's confession, then the narration re-translates that meaning into abstract moral summary. 'Forgiveness would have been a transaction' and the explanatory recap of Lena's trust overstate what the scene has already made legible, and they push Kai's close-third voice toward essayed judgment instead of his more concrete, oblique register.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:247 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:249 so the aftermath runs through Kai's body, memory, and exit rather than a conceptual gloss on forgiveness. Preserve the state shift already achieved at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:243 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:245, but let the scene leave on concrete pressure instead of paraphrased ethics.
findings.acceptance_test: After the revised confession beat, the passage reaches Kai's departure without abstractly defining forgiveness or restating Lena's vulnerability in thesis language. The revision passes only if a cold reader can still identify the same state shift from the scene action and final exchange alone.

summary: Chapter 15 keeps continuity and narrative tense consistent with the provided materials, and it does land the planned aftermath beats. It still fails this review because the Kai confrontation depends on an unearned knowledge leap, Margot's addiction is explained more than bodily felt, both confrontations reuse the same interrogation ladder, and the prose repeatedly over-solves emphasis through negation scaffolds and post-landing moral paraphrase.
